blocks.text:
To: Joseph Wallace
Cc: Jason Torres, Mary Parker

Thank you. The acquired-company accounts came through, and Mary Parker has already started folding them into the baseline. That closes the last major gap in the data for {{fact:f:E-2009-003.client}}, and it means we can bring you a full view of the combined cost base at the review rather than a partial one.

Ahead of that, I am sending over a short briefing deck so you and your team can see where the work stands before we walk you through it. It is deliberately light on conclusions. We are early, and Jason Torres wants you to see the shape of the analysis and the questions still open, not a headline the data does not yet support.

One request when your finance staff have a moment: please have them confirm the account definitions Jason Torres circulated, so that the Carroll, Merritt and Williams figures and the acquired-company figures are built on the same basis. The main thing to settle is how each company treats overhead, since the two do it differently and that alone changes the combined figure. It is the one item that could still move the baseline.
